name: monitor-guard
description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for background / long-running shell launches (trailing `&` · `nohup`/`setsid`/`disown` · the Bash tool's `run_in_background` flag). When the rate-limit-survivable pattern is incomplete, emits non-blocking additionalContext enforcing commons @D g10 — the while-monitor pattern: (1) detach the job (`nohup`/`setsid`) so a session rate-limit death does NOT SIGHUP it, (2) write a progress log (`> /tmp/<job>.log 2>&1`) as a durable record + Monitor re-attach point, (3) attach the 🛰️ Monitor tool to the LOG (not the live pipe — token-light, re-attachable after a rate-limit), with an OS-side `while` heartbeat for liveness (no model-side sleep-poll). Stays silent when the job is already detached AND logged. Closes the rate-limit-force-kill class for general local background work; general-local sister of pod-monitor (which covers `hexa cloud` GPU pods). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_monitor_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.1.1 — `hexa cloud nohup`/`hexa cloud fire` patterns ALWAYS fire a cloud-bridge hint (even when the basic detach+log invariant is satisfied, since the cloud workflow has a SEPARATE requirement: the caller must thread the logfile path from nohup into `hexa cloud tail` so Monitor can attach). 0.1.2 — the `hexa cloud fire` verb + `__MONITOR_HANDLE__={…}` JSON-line contract are now SHIPPED upstream (hexa-lang PR #1306 + #1309); cloud-bridge hint now points to the live atomic workflow (`hexa cloud fire <host> -- <argv>` → grep handle line → attach Monitor to `tail_cmd`) instead of the pre-ship 3-step manual handoff. NO opt-out by design.
